title: Microsoft 365 - Reduced access to services due to an affiliation change
body: Please note, this document is not intended to do the following: Recommend an affiliation type a user needs to gain access to a service they would not be eligible for. Determine why a user is losing access to certain services. Advise HR departments or the Registrar's office on how to change a user’s affiliation type. This document does not apply to individuals who leave and have no affiliation with the university (e.g., faculty/staff no longer employed by UW-Madison or students). These individuals will receive deactivation notifications . Backing up your data Leaving the University of Wisconsin-Madison Leaving the University recommendations for HR/Managers/IT Support/Collaborators Reduced access email notifications The reduced access email notifications will include the following information: awareness of upcoming access change to Microsoft 365 services current affiliation group new affiliation group date when the change will go into effect The following are some examples of scenarios when affiliation changes will trigger the series of reduced access email notifications: faculty retires and receives continued access to campus IT services faculty receives emeritus status former student Example of reduced access email notification From: Productivity and Collaborative Solutions <pcs@mailplus.wisc.edu> Sent: May 27, 2020 12:42 PM To: Bucky Badger <bbadger@wisc.edu> Subject: Your access to UW-Madison Office 365 services is changing soon Hello Bucky, Your access to UW-Madison Microsoft 365 services will change on August 24, 2020. On August 24, 2020, the UW-Madison Office 365 license for your NetID "bbadger" will change from group A to Group D. Learn more about this group change here: https://kb.wisc.edu/62576. You will continue to have your current level of access to UW-Madison Office 365 services until this date. Before August 24, 2020: - Review the services that change for your account - Backup or transfer ownership of data If you have any questions, please contact the DoIT Help Desk at https://it.wisc.edu/help. Thank you, UW-Madison Office 365 Team Productivity and Collaborative Solutions (PCS) Division of Information Technology (DoIT) University of Wisconsin-Madison Affiliation groups and access to Microsoft 365 services Eligibility for M365 accounts and applications is determined by a combination of factors including vendor contract agreements and campus funding decisions. University affiliation is determined by your faculty/staff appointment type and/or current student status. If you have questions regarding your current affiliation with UW-Madison, please contact the Office of Human Resources or your departmental HR office. Eligibility Group Definitions (subject to change): Groups A and B: Current UW-Madison Faculty/Staff with PAID Appointments Currently Enrolled UW-Madison Students Student Employees Groups C: Contractors/Consultants Faculty/Staff with Zero-dollar Appointments (specifically designated by HR) Groups D and E: Former Student Retirees Emeritus Professors Zero-dollar Appointments Important: Storage Quota Changes If your affiliation changes from Groups A/B to Groups C/D/E, your storage quota for email and OneDrive for Business will be reduced and cannot be increased. Please ensure your usage is below your allocated quota for both services before the transition. Email (Groups C/D/E): Reduced from 100 GB to 50 GB. Manage email usage . OneDrive for Business (Group C only, Groups D/E are ineligible): Reduced from 2 TB to a minimum of 25 GB and up to 100 GB. Manage OneDrive usage . Microsoft 365 Eligibility Groups eligibility groups Group A B C D E M365 Services/Applications Exchange Online Only* X X X Exchange Online X X Personal Bookings X X Forms X X X Office Pro Plus ** (M365 Desktop Applications) X X M365 Web Apps X X X OneDrive for Business X X X PowerApps X X X SharePoint Online X X X Teams X X X To-Do X X X * Exchange Online provides access to email, calendar, people, contacts, and tasks via the Web Client ( link ) **Office Pro Plus includes desktop versions of Access, Excel, OneNote, Outlook, PowerPoint, and Publisher See Also Leaving the University of Wisconsin-Madison - Access to IT Services Help Desk - Leaving the University recommendations for HR/Managers/IT Support/Collaborators Microsoft 365 - What features are included in Microsoft 365?
